title: Off to Sea from Sally Forth Red Cross Book
display_title: Off to Sea from Sally Forth Red Cross Book
wikitext:
[]: ''Off to Sea'' from ''Sally Forth'' (Red Cross Book)             Quote Post by ssevangelion1995 » Thu Apr 02, 2020 2:35 pm In the Seiyuu-retrospective section of the EofE Red Cross Book, in her retrospective, Miyamura Yuuko alludes to a song called ''Off to Sea'' derived from a work called ''Sally Forth'' (I regret my study of the language is in its infancy, these are the translations provided by the English version offered here - https://evangelion.fandom.com/wiki/Red_Cross_Book ). Could a more experienced hand find these for me? English Google is thus far not successful. Is it perhaps a gunka , a patriotic military song?

[]: Re: ''Off to Sea'' from ''Sally Forth'' (Red Cross Book)             Quote Post by ssevangelion1995 » Sat Apr 04, 2020 1:21 pm Edit - I ought, I think, have used the properly polite and deferential form Miyamura Yuuko-san . I apologise most sincerely - I had no desire to be disrespectful and the bumptious foreign outsider is rightly deplored in any culture, especially, I imagine, in one as ancient, beautiful and formal as that of Japan. This is a digression, but is there any guidance on correct etiquette for Japanese names in an Internet setting? I ought to begin so as to go on politely and with due deference, courtesy and respect or else I am hardly any better than a barbarian.

chunks.content:
Article: Off to Sea from Sally Forth Red Cross Book
[]: Off to Sea from Sally Forth (Red Cross Book)             Quote Post by ssevangelion1995 » Thu Apr 02, 2020 2:35 pm In the Seiyuu-retrospective section of the EofE Red Cross Book, in her retrospective, Miyamura Yuuko alludes to a song called Off to Sea derived from a work called Sally Forth (I regret my study of the language is in its infancy, these are the translations provided by the English version offered here - https://evangelion.fandom.com/wiki/Red_Cross_Book ). Could a more experienced hand find these for me? English Google is thus far not successful. Is it perhaps a gunka , a patriotic military song?

[]: Re: Off to Sea from Sally Forth (Red Cross Book)             Quote Post by ssevangelion1995 » Sat Apr 04, 2020 1:21 pm Edit - I ought, I think, have used the properly polite and deferential form Miyamura Yuuko-san . I apologise most sincerely - I had no desire to be disrespectful and the bumptious foreign outsider is rightly deplored in any culture, especially, I imagine, in one as ancient, beautiful and formal as that of Japan. This is a digression, but is there any guidance on correct etiquette for Japanese names in an Internet setting? I ought to begin so as to go on politely and with due deference, courtesy and respect or else I am hardly any better than a barbarian.
